zh.Horbling, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾霍爾布林
zh.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen於1400年出生在英國林可爾史爾林肯。她於大約1415年與William Pykworth在英國林可爾史爾林肯結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1500年在她的家鄉享年100歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾林肯。

zh-hans.Horbling, Lincolnshire, England: 英国英格兰林可爾史爾霍爾布林
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen于1400年出生在英国林可爾史爾林肯。她于大约1415年与William Pykworth在英国林可爾史爾林肯结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1500年在她的家乡享年100岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾林肯。

nameMeanings.name: Allen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Allenby
meanings: English: habitational name from Allonby (Cumberland). The placename is a post-Conquest compound of the French (Breton) personal name Alein (see Allen ) + Old Norse bȳ ‘village’, or the same word borrowed into Middle English.

variants.name: Allin
meanings: English (Devon): variant of   Allen  .
Swedish: variant of   Ahlin  .

variants.name: Alano
meanings: Italian (mainly Campania) and Hispanic (mainly Philippines): from the personal name Alano (see Allen ).

variants.name: Alan
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Allen  . This is the usual form of the personal name in England and Scotland.

variants.name: Allan
meanings: Scottish and northern English: variant of   Allen  . This is the more common form of the name in Scotland and northern England. See also   McAllen  .

History: An early bearer of this surname in North America was William Allan, a British soldier who took his family to the newly founded city of Halifax, NS, Canada, in 1749. His son John (1746–1805) was a Revolutionary soldier who eventually (1783) founded the settlement of Allan's Island, Passamaquoddy Bay, ME.

variants.name: Allain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Jean-Paul, Adelard, Alban, Albenie, Alphonse, Andree, Cyrille, Donat, Emile, Euclide, Francois.

Breton and French: from the Old Breton personal name Alan (Old French Alain); see Allen . In France the personal name is spelled Alain, and this is also a less common variant of the surname (see Alain ).
English: variant of   Allen  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Alen
meanings: English: variant of   Allen  .
Galician (Alén): habitational name from one of the many places called Alén in Galicia (Spain), from Galician alén ‘beyond’.
Flemish and Dutch: patronymic from   Ale  .

variants.name: Allis
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Alis, Alice (Old French Adeliz, Ahelis, Aalis, Aliz). Alice, together with its pet form Alison, was one of the most popular names in both England and Scotland from the 13th century until the end of the medieval period. The personal name is of ancient Germanic origin, brought to England from France by the Normans; it is a shortened form of ancient Germanic Adalheidis, which is composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + haid ‘‘rank, worth’’.

variants.name: Alleyne
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Chantal, Julien, Marcell.

English: variant of   Allen  . This is an old-fashioned spelling, already established as a surname in England in Tudor times. Today this surname is found mainly in the West Indies, especially Barbados.

variants.name: Alling
meanings: English: either a variant of Allen , or a variant of Halling with loss of initial H-.
German: habitational name from either of two places called Alling, one in Bavaria and one in Austria.
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Alling. The placename may be a derivative of al ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen was born in 1400, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England. She married William Pykworth about 1415, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in 1500, in her hometown, at the age of 100, and was buried in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen est née en 1400, à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé William Pykworth environ 1415, à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1500, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 100 ans, et a été enterrée à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen wurde 1400 in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England geboren. Sie hat um 1415 in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England William Pykworth geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1500 im Alter von 100 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen è nata il nel 1400 nel Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England. Lei sposato William Pykworth circa 1415, nel Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì nel 1500, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 100 anni, e fu sepolta a Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Allenは1400年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンで生まれた。約1415年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンでWilliam Pykworthと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1500年に故郷で100歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアリンコルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen(은)는 1400년 영국 링코실르 링코컨에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1415년 영국 링코실르 링코컨에서 William Pykworth(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1500년 그녀의 고향에서 100세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 링코컨에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen nasceu em 1400, em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com William Pykworth aproximadamente 1415, em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1500, em sua cidade natal, com 100 anos, e foi sepultada em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen родилась в 1400 г. в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Pykworth около 1415 г. в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1500 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 100 лет и была похоронена в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Agnes Allen nació en 1400, en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con William Pykworth aproximadamente 1415, en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió en 1500, en su pueblo, a la edad de 100 años, y fue sepultada en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
